paper_title: Spatial Distribution, Ecological Risk Assessment and Source Analysis of Heavy Metals Pollution in Urban Lake Sediments of Huaihe River Basin

methods.description: Principal component analysis (PCA), the absolute principal-component-score (APCS), and multiple-linear regression (MLR) were coupled to trace the heavy metals in lake sediments in the study area.

steps.description: Standardize the concentration data of heavy-metal elements and introduce a zero-concentration factor.
steps.searchable_summary: standardize heavy metal concentration data and introduce zero-concentration factor

steps.description: Calculate the PCA normalization factor fraction minus Z0 to obtain the absolute principal component submultiple (APCS).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate APCS using PCA normalization factor

steps.description: Using APCS as an independent variable and the heavy-metal concentration as the dependent variable, multiple linear regression was carried out, and the source contribution of each heavy metal was calculated by the regression coefficient.
steps.searchable_summary: perform MLR using APCS and heavy metal concentration to calculate source contribution

methods.description: Analysis of the correlation between heavy metals in sediments to determine if they share common sources.

steps.description: Determine the correlation between any two of the four elements Cr, Zn, Ni, and Cu in the sediments of the study area.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate correlation coefficients for Cr, Zn, Ni, Cu
inputs: sediment data for Cr, Zn, Ni, Cu

steps.description: Determine the correlation between any two of the four elements Pb, Cd, As, and Hg.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate correlation coefficients for Pb, Cd, As, Hg
inputs: sediment data for Pb, Cd, As, Hg

methods.description: Principal component analysis to identify the main sources of heavy metals in sediments.

steps.description: Examine eight heavy metals by KMO and the Bartlett spherical test to determine data significance.
steps.searchable_summary: perform KMO and Bartlett test on heavy metal data

steps.description: Use Kaiser's standardized orthogonal rotation method to extract two principal components.
steps.searchable_summary: extract principal components using Kaiser's method

methods.description: The principal component analysis/absolute principal component fraction receptor model and the multiple linear regression equation (PCA/APCS-MLR) were used to quantify the contribution rates of two provenances to the heavy-metal concentrations in sediments.

steps.description: Use PCA/APCS-MLR to quantify the contribution rates of two provenances to the heavy-metal concentrations in sediments.
steps.searchable_summary: quantify provenance contribution rates using PCA/APCS-MLR
